steps.title: Graph the vertex form
steps.content: The vertex is $(-3,6)$, so the model has the form $y=a(x+3)^2+6$. Use the known x-intercept $\left(-\frac{17}{4},0\right)$ and the vertex point $(-3,6)$ in Desmos, then fit $y_1\sim a(x_1+3)^2+6$.

steps.title: Mirror the known root across the axis
steps.content:
Other intercept $= 2h - r_1 = 2(-3) - \left(-\dfrac{17}{4}\right) = -6 + \dfrac{17}{4} = -\dfrac{24}{4} + \dfrac{17}{4} = -\dfrac{7}{4}$.
The other x-intercept is $\left(-\dfrac{7}{4}, 0\right)$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
